metrics.narrative: The core pain is well quantified qualitatively: a 'clunky' Proactis purchase-to-pay system with no robust contract storage, no automated renewal alerts, and heavy manual invoice/PO matching (Bright Horizons attendee, 2 Sep 2025 call). During the May-Jun 2026 sandbox POC, invoice line-item extraction hit a 97% success rate (9 Jun 2026, Zycus/AP drop-in). Zycus also offered an illustrative 30x year-one ROI case (26 Mar 2026), but this is Zycus's own estimate, not a figure Bright Horizons has validated or owned.

sources: Avoma calls 18 May - 9 Jun 2026 (sandbox POC series)
champion.narrative: No single named internal advocate has been confirmed defending Zycus against a late price challenge. Andrew Duckworth holds clear decision authority and has voiced strong appreciation ('expressed strong appreciation for the end-to-end demo,' 10 Jul 2025), and Elaine Rymill has run point operationally across essentially every 2026 touchpoint -- scheduling the sandbox drop-ins, chasing tender corrections (3 Mar 2026), and coordinating feedback -- functioning as Andrew Duckworth's operational co-champion. What is still missing is evidence either of them is actively selling Zycus internally above themselves (e.g., to Joanne Urso or finance) on value versus price.

competition.narrative: Proactis is the incumbent (contract renewal discussed 2 Sep 2025, described by the buyer as 'clunky' with skepticism it can offer a competitive alternative). GEP and Coupa were named earlier in the qualification process; iCertis and SIREN Labs surfaced as CLM-specific alternatives at the 16 May 2025 discovery call. The Next Step's 'POC was successful - best Platform' line suggests Zycus has effectively cleared the field, but no fresh competitive intelligence (final shortlist composition, rival pricing) has been captured since the formal RFP concluded.

identify_pain.narrative: Pain is well articulated and consistent across a year of calls: multiple disjointed systems (Proactis for PO/invoice capture, manual supplier onboarding), no robust contract storage or renewal alerts, SQL-only reporting with no self-service dashboarding, and a desire for one Workday-integrated platform across UK and (eventually) US operations (2 Sep 2025 call, 16 May 2025 discovery call).

sources: Avoma manifest: 24 Apr 2026 and 3 Jun 2026 (both not_recorded)
paper_process.narrative: The Next Step points straight at 'Contract Redlining and Signing July,' but Bright Horizons' first round of MSA redlines has not yet been sent (open buyer commitment), and OneTrust vendor-security-questionnaire access -- an explicit condition of the award -- is still outstanding. Zycus has committed to arrange a legal-to-legal call as soon as redlines land, and still owes Elaine Rymill answers to her OneTrust references-section questions. Legal Counsel Catherine Wilson has attended two sessions (24 Apr and 3 Jun 2026) but neither was recorded, so her specific concerns are unread.

economic_buyer.narrative: Andrew Duckworth (Director of Procurement, Decision Maker) has repeatedly demonstrated commercial authority: on the 10 Jul 2025 call he 'confirmed a follow-up discussion on commercial model and solution integration, indicating his ultimate decision authority.' Joanne Urso (VP Procurement, also a Decision Maker per Salesforce) engaged only once, at the 16 May 2025 kickoff demo, where she 'highlighted the interest in a globally scalable procurement system' -- she has not appeared as an attendee on any subsequent touchpoint through 9 Jul 2026, so her active sponsorship above Andrew Duckworth is unconfirmed.

decision_process.narrative: The process ran discovery (16 May 2025) -> tailored demos (23 May, 10 Jul 2025) -> pricing/phasing discussion (2 Sep 2025) -> formal RFP/tender (Jan-Mar 2026) -> a further deep-dive presentation (26 Mar 2026) -> sandbox POC across five department drop-ins (18 May-9 Jun 2026) -> a vendor-selection call and commercial pack (per current Next Step) -> contract redlining and signing targeted for Jul 2026. The sequence and named participants at each stage are well evidenced.

items.name: Award Subject to Contract notification
items.summary: Elaine Rymill (BH Procurement) formally notified Anthony Gray that Zycus won the tender, subject to contract and OneTrust security/privacy onboarding, sharing scores (82% overall, 87/100 on demo) and asking Zycus to provide a detailed delivery timeline/programme.

items.name: Anthony's award acceptance
items.summary: Anthony Gray thanked Elaine for the win, confirmed the team is moving straight into contract and SOW phase, and invited BH to flag anything needed in the interim — no reply pending, just relationship-building.

items.name: Post-award scope clarification
items.summary: Elaine clarified to Anthony that the award covers UK only (per prior discussion) and flagged an NDA is being sent to cover work while the MSA/SOW are finalized; she also noted BH is still waiting on unspecified items Zycus was to confirm from last week — an open action on Zycus's side.

items.name: NDA sent for review/signature routing
items.summary: Elaine sent the bilateral NDA to Anthony asking him to confirm contracting entity (UK branch of the Indian entity), verify the Indian address sourced from BH's website, and confirm UK VAT registration, wanting it signed before the following week.

items.name: Anthony confirms NDA ready to sign
items.summary: Anthony replied that Zycus legal confirmed the NDA is bilateral and ready, asking Elaine to route it via DocuSign (or their own platform) for signature and countersignature.

items.name: Elaine pushes back for outstanding answers
items.summary: Elaine noted Anthony hadn't yet answered her specific questions (entity, address, VAT) and asked him to respond so BH legal can proceed — signature is now blocked on Zycus providing those details.

items.name: Zycus contracting package delivered
items.summary: Zycus sent Elaine the Supplier Onboarding form (with BH's registration number), the MSA (also placed in Showpad) requesting redlines within a week to enable a legal-to-legal call, and confirmed the SOW workshop booked for 15 July, while raising questions about the mandatory reference-contact fields on the onboarding form.

items.name: SOW workshop scheduling thread
items.summary: Elaine and Nishita Sareen (Zycus Solutions Consultant) went back and forth finalizing the first SOW workshop for 2pm on 15 July, with Elaine asking who from Zycus would attend/run it and Nishita looping in Hrishikesh Pachhapur as her availability was constrained by PTO.

day_summary.overall: This was the deal's biggest day yet: Bright Horizons formally awarded the Source-to-Pay/Contract Software tender to Zycus (subject to contract), with strong scores (82% overall, 87/100 on demos), and both sides immediately pivoted into contracting mode. Zycus sent over the MSA, Supplier Onboarding form, and NDA in parallel, while procurement (Elaine) and Zycus (Anthony/Nishita) locked in the first SOW scoping workshop for 15 July. Ball is now mostly in Zycus's court to answer onboarding/reference questions and get the NDA signed, while Bright Horizons' legal team works redlines on the MSA.

north_star_verdict.math: Today is 2026-07-07; close date is 2026-07-31, 24 days out. The opportunity has been open since 2025-05-27 (~14 months) and has sat in Shortlisted through a formal RFP, POC sandbox (18 May-9 Jun 2026), and vendor-selection call (6 Jul 2026, yesterday). Next_Step__c (last modified 2026-07-01) targets 'contract redlining and signing' within July, immediately following vendor selection, but no legal/redline activity is evidenced in the reviewed manifest, making the remaining runway tight for an enterprise MSA/SOW cycle.
evidence: Vendor-selection call held 2026-07-06 (yesterday); buyer sent a same-day email reply (Deal Pulse) - the first confirmed two-way contact in ~3 weeks.
Next_Step__c: 'POC was successful - best Platform' (SF, last modified 2026-07-01).
No MSA/SOW redline exchange evidenced in the manifest ahead of the July signing target.
Economic Buyer Joanne Urso last seen on a call 2025-05-16, ~14 months before this sweep.
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus's POC was validated as 'best Platform' and the vendor-selection call closed yesterday (6 Jul 2026) with a same-day buyer email reply, but with 24 days left to the 31 Jul close, no contract redlining or legal exchange has yet been evidenced.

flags: No champion identified: no internal advocate to counter price objections or promote Zycus platform superiority and AI differentiation at vendor selection gate Monday 2026-07-08. Securing Executive Sponsor (Julie Sheppard) or developing Andrew Duckworth/Elaine Rymill as advocates is immediate priority.

Economic Buyer (Joanne Urso, VP Procurement) engagement and sponsorship level unknown due to zero Avoma calls. Risk of passive approval vs. active sponsorship. If Joanne is passive and Andrew/Elaine are price-focused without ROI anchor, competitor can win on price despite POC superiority.
NO QUANTIFIED BUSINESS CASE: No ROI, savings target, or efficiency metrics documented. Technical POC win without financial value anchor creates risk of price-driven decision over platform quality.

Zero competitive intelligence despite formal vendor selection process tomorrow. No competitor names, shortlist composition, pricing intel, or buyer preference drivers known. Blind to competitor positioning. If competitor undercuts and we lack ROI anchor or champion advocacy, we lose on price despite POC technical win.
Zero Avoma buyer calls discovered post-POC; no visibility into selection committee alignment, commercial feedback status, or late-stage objections 2 days before vendor selection decision

Treat the 14-16 Jul SOW workshop outcome, not the 31 Jul placeholder date, as the true signature predictor — Zycus SOWs are signed separately by the AVP Global Delivery and buyers routinely won't sign until scope is locked.

Reinforce the Merlin Intake AI/agentic narrative with Andrew before the SOW locks scope — Zycus won largely on price and platform fit, and cementing the AI value story now protects the Phase 2 expansion and defends against later scope-trimming.
With vendor selection complete, get Catherine Wilson (Legal Counsel) actively redlining the MSA/SOW immediately to protect the 31 Jul close.

Re-engage the economic buyer directly: Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) has not had direct Zycus contact since the 16 May 2025 kickoff call; get her visible sign-off before signature to remove single-thread risk sitting on Andrew Duckworth alone.

Close the pricing objection gap: the 18 Jun 2026 commercial-feedback deadline has passed with no confirmed resolution recorded, and no buyer-validated ROI number exists beyond Zycus's own illustrative 30x estimate (26 Mar 2026) -- anchor the final commercial ask to a number Bright Horizons owns before redlining locks in.

Build a named internal champion beyond Elaine Rymill's coordination role: with the vendor-selection call already run and 'best Platform' recorded, no one has been confirmed as actively defending Zycus against a late price challenge inside Bright Horizons.
best_practice_check.summary: The deal's technical win is well earned; the remaining risk is entirely commercial, security/legal, and single-thread, all compressible into the 22 days left before close.

customer_preference.evidence: Next Step (current): 'POC was successful - best Platform.' Andrew Duckworth 'expressed strong appreciation for the end-to-end demo and its integrated approach across sourcing, contracting, and AP' (10 Jul 2025). AP's Stuart Lamont: 'it looks fine to me... it really helps having familiar suppliers in there' (9 Jun 2026).

competitive_position.summary: Proactis, the incumbent P2P/invoicing system, has been the primary displacement target since at least Sep 2025, described by the buyer as 'clunky' with skepticism it could offer a competitive alternative. GEP and Coupa were named early in the process, and iCertis/SIREN Labs surfaced as CLM-specific alternatives at first discovery. The current Next Step ('POC was successful - best Platform') indicates Zycus has cleared the field, but no fresh competitive intelligence has been captured since the formal RFP concluded, so threat levels below reflect the last dated evidence, not a fresh re-check.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: The underlying deal facts are strong -- a formal RFP won, a multi-department sandbox POC declared 'best Platform,' and dated forward motion into redlining -- but the recorded stage lags reality, the economic buyer above Andrew Duckworth has been silent for over a year, no named internal champion has emerged, and the commercial-feedback and paperwork gates that must clear inside the remaining 22 days have not yet moved on the buyer's side.

deal_risk.text: 22 days to close with sequential gates unstarted -- MSA redlines, the OneTrust security review, and final signature all still need to happen inside the 31 Jul 2026 close date, with no confirmed forcing-function deadline to anchor urgency.

No buyer-validated ROI figure -- the only quantified business case on record is Zycus's own illustrative 30x year-one ROI estimate (26 Mar 2026); Bright Horizons has not confirmed its own savings number.

Legal engagement real but unread -- Catherine Wilson (Legal Counsel) attended two sessions (24 Apr and 3 Jun 2026) that were not recorded, so her specific redline concerns are unconfirmed going into contracting.

win_position.text: POC declared the winning platform -- the Next Step log states 'POC was successful - best Platform,' after months of sandbox testing across procurement, AP, legal, IT and nursery-operations users (18 May - 9 Jun 2026).

Deep, multi-department engagement -- 23 buyer touchpoints since 16 May 2025 spanning discovery, tailored demos, a formal RFP/tender (Jan-Mar 2026), and department-by-department POC drop-ins through Jun 2026.

Salesforce stage (Shortlisted) lags the deal's real position -- the Next Step already describes a completed vendor-selection call and points straight to 'Contract Redlining and Signing July,' a Vendor-Selected/Negotiation-stage motion, not a Shortlisted one.

Economic buyer thin above Andrew Duckworth -- Joanne Urso (VP Procurement) has had no direct Zycus contact since the 16 May 2025 kickoff call, over 13 months ago; her active sponsorship at signature is unconfirmed.

deal_momentum.text: Genuine two-way buyer contact 1 day ago -- a buyer email reply landed 8 Jul 2026, the freshest verified buyer touch on record.

Dated forward plan in place -- the Next Step names a completed vendor-selection call, then commercial feedback, then 'Contract Redlining and Signing July,' giving the deal a concrete near-term cadence.
Commercial feedback is 21 days overdue -- the 18 Jun 2026 deadline for Andrew Duckworth and Elaine Rymill's feedback passed with no confirmed resolution logged.
Buyer-owed paperwork not yet moving -- Bright Horizons' first MSA redlines and its OneTrust security-questionnaire access (an explicit award condition) are both still outstanding.

customer_commitment.text: Extensive time investment -- Bright Horizons ran a multi-week sandbox POC (18 May - 9 Jun 2026) across five separate department drop-in sessions (procurement, AP, legal, nurseries, US), a level of hands-on commitment rarely seen this early.

Positive end-user feedback -- a nursery manager praised the basket/favourites features and AP's Stuart Lamont called the invoice-matching workflow fine and helpful with familiar suppliers cached in (2 Jun and 9 Jun 2026 drop-ins).

No named champion beyond the buying team itself -- Andrew Duckworth holds decision authority and Elaine Rymill has run point operationally, but no one has been confirmed as actively defending Zycus internally against a late price challenge.
